workflow.name: Schedule and confirm revenue ops meetings with Pipedrive, Google Calendar and Slack
workflow.description:
## Categories  
**CRM Automation, Revenue Operations, Sales Operations, Meeting Automation**

---

## Build a Revenue Ops Meeting Pipeline with Pipedrive, Calendar, Slack

This workflow creates a **CRM-driven revenue operations meeting pipeline** that automatically coordinates meetings once a deal reaches a specific stage in **Pipedrive**.

When a deal moves into the *Meeting Booking* stage, the workflow waits for the SDR to complete the meeting details, creates the event in **Google Calendar**, sends a confirmation email to the client, and notifies the internal team in **Slack**.

The result is a reliable, no-manual-work system that ensures meetings are scheduled, confirmed, and communicated without human follow-up.

---

## Benefits

- **CRM as the Single Source of Truth**  
  All automation is triggered directly from deal stage changes.

- **Reduced No-Shows**  
  Clients receive timely meeting confirmations with correct links and times.

- **Zero Manual Coordination**  
  No copying links, sending reminders, or checking calendars.

- **Internal Visibility**  
  Sales teams receive Slack reminders automatically.

- **Time-Zone Safe Scheduling**  
  Meeting times are calculated and normalized automatically.

---

## How It Works

### Deal Stage Trigger (Pipedrive)

- Listens for deal updates in Pipedrive  
- Runs only when the deal enters the *Meeting Booking* stage  
- Prevents execution on irrelevant pipeline changes

---

### Controlled Wait Logic

- Pauses execution to allow the SDR to:
  - Add the meeting link
  - Set date, time, and duration
- Ensures data completeness before scheduling

---

### Data Extraction & Enrichment

- Fetches full deal details
- Extracts:
  - Client name and email
  - Company name
  - Meeting link
  - Meeting date and time
- Calculates ISO start and end times with time-zone handling

---

### Calendar Event Creation (Google Calendar)

- Creates a calendar event automatically
- Adds the client as an attendee
- Inserts the meeting link as the event location

---

### Client Email Confirmation

- Sends a personalized confirmation email
- Includes meeting date, time, and context
- Reduces rescheduling and confusion

---

### Internal Slack Notification

- Sends a reminder to a selected Slack channel
- Notifies SDRs and sales managers of upcoming meetings
- Keeps teams aligned without CRM checking

---

## Required Setup

### Pipedrive
- Deal pipeline with a defined *Meeting Booking* stage  
- Meeting details stored in deal activities  

### Google Calendar
- OAuth access enabled  
- Permission to create events  

### Gmail
- OAuth access enabled  
- Email sending permissions  

### Slack
- OAuth access enabled  
- Target channel selected  

---

## Business Use Cases

### Sales Teams
- Eliminate missed meetings and manual reminders  
- Reduce admin work for SDRs  

### Revenue Operations
- Standardize meeting execution across pipelines  
- Improve forecasting reliability  

### Founders & Managers
- Increase meeting attendance without micromanagement  

### Agencies & Consultants
- Deliver CRM-based RevOps automation to clients  

---

## Difficulty Level  
**Intermediate**

---

## Estimated Build Time  
**45–60 minutes**

---

## Monthly Operating Cost

- Pipedrive: Existing plan  
- Google Calendar: Free  
- Gmail: Free  
- Slack: Free or paid workspace  
- n8n: Self-hosted or cloud  

Typical range: **$0–20/month**

---

## Why This Workflow Works

- Deal stage changes represent real sales intent  
- Waiting logic prevents broken automations  
- Calendar-first execution ensures reliability  
- Multi-channel notifications reduce human error  

---

## Possible Extensions

- Add SMS or WhatsApp reminders  
- Auto-cancel meetings on stage rollback  
- Log meeting outcomes back to Pipedrive  
- Trigger post-meeting follow-ups  
- Add AI-generated meeting summaries

Hope this message finds you will.

This email regarding our online meeting to discuss more about our product.

Kindly find the meeting time at {{ DateTime.fromISO($json.start.dateTime).toFormat('dd LLL yyyy, hh:mm a') }}

Regards,

parameters.text:

Meeting Link: {{ $('Create a meeting and add you recipient').item.json.location }}

parameters.content:
## 📌 Template Overview (Read Before Using)

### What is it
###### This template is a **Revenue Ops meeting automation** that connects Pipedrive, Google Calendar, Gmail, and Slack. 

###### When a deal reaches the *Meeting Booking* stage in Pipedrive, the workflow automatically schedules the meeting, confirms it with the client, and notifies the internal team.  

###### It is designed to eliminate manual coordination and reduce missed or forgotten meetings.


---

### How it works
1. The workflow listens for **deal stage changes** in Pipedrive.  
2. It runs only when the deal enters the *Meeting Booking* stage.  
3. A short wait is applied to allow the SDR to add meeting details.  
4. Deal and activity data are extracted and time values are normalized.  
5. A meeting is created in Google Calendar with the client as an attendee.  
6. A confirmation email is sent to the client via Gmail.  
7. A reminder is posted in a Slack channel for the sales team.

---

### Setup steps
1. Connect your **Pipedrive account** and confirm the correct stage ID for *Meeting Booking*.  
2. Ensure meeting details (date, time, duration, meeting link) are saved in the deal activity.  
3. Connect **Google Calendar** and select the calendar where meetings should be created.  
4. Connect **Gmail** and review the email message content.  
5. Connect **Slack** and choose the channel for internal reminders.  
6. Run the workflow once with test data to confirm timings and notifications are correct.  
7. Activate the workflow once validation is complete.

### Pipedrive Trigger
**Why it exists**  
Listens for deal updates so automation runs only when sales activity actually happens.

**You can change**  
- Pipeline ID  
- Stage ID (for example, use a different stage like *Demo Scheduled*)  
- Trigger entity (deal vs activity)

### Wait Node
**Why it exists**  
Prevents the workflow from running before the SDR finishes adding meeting details.

**You can change**  
- Wait time duration  
- Replace with a conditional check instead of time-based waiting  
- Remove it if meeting data is always available immediately

### IF Stage Check
**Why it exists**  
Ensures the workflow only runs for qualified deals and avoids unwanted executions.

**You can change**  
- Stage ID value  
- Add multiple allowed stages  
- Add extra conditions like deal value or owner

### Extract Deal Info
**Why it exists**  
Pulls complete and reliable data directly from Pipedrive.

**You can change**  
- Fields extracted from the deal  
- Add custom fields  
- Include organization or owner data

### Set / Transform Data
**Why it exists**  
Cleans and prepares data so downstream tools receive correct formats.

**You can change**  
- Time zone  
- Date and time calculations  
- Field names for CRM or calendar compatibility

### Create Calendar Event
**Why it exists**  
Creates a single source of truth for the meeting and avoids double booking.

**You can change**  
- Calendar selection  
- Meeting title and description  
- Replace Google Calendar with Outlook

### Send Email Reminder
**Why it exists**  
Confirms the meeting with the client and reduces no-shows.

**You can change**  
- Email wording  
- Sender account  
- Add attachments or CC internal users

### Send Slack Notification
**Why it exists**  
Keeps the sales team informed without checking the CRM.

**You can change**  
- Target channel  
- Message format  
- Replace Slack with Microsoft Teams or Discord

Founders and ops teams use me to replace manual work with reliable n8n systems. Manual work is costing you money every day. Every week you delay makes the problem harder and more expensive.

👉 Book a n8n build or training call.

data.details:
The IF node can be used to implement binary conditional logic in your workflow. You can set up one-to-many conditions to evaluate each item of data being inputted into the node. That data will either evaluate to TRUE or FALSE and route out of the node accordingly.

This node has multiple types of conditions: Bool, String, Number, and Date & Time.
